title: Vegetable Jhalfrezi
time: Prep Time: 20 mins | Cook Time: 25 mins

ingredients.title: For the base sauce
items: 2 tbsp neutral oil (sunflower, canola, or light olive oil)
2–3 green chillies, slit lengthwise (adjust to taste)
1 tsp Kashmiri red chilli powder (for color & mild heat)
½ tsp crushed fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi)

ingredients.title: For the vegetables
items: 1 medium carrot, cut into thin batons
½ cup green beans, cut into 2-inch pieces
½ cup paneer cubes (optional, lightly pan-seared)

instruction.title: 1. Prepare the base masala
steps: Heat oil in a heavy-bottomed pan or kadhai over medium heat.
Add cumin seeds and let them crackle.
Add sliced onions and sauté until light golden (6–8 minutes).
Stir in ginger–garlic paste, sauté until raw aroma disappears.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until they break down into a thick paste.
Mix in tomato purée and cook for 2 minutes for depth of flavor.

instruction.title: 2. Add spices
steps: Add ground coriander, ground cumin, turmeric, Kashmiri chilli powder, and salt.
Cook the masala until oil begins to separate at the edges — a key step for rich flavor.

instruction.title: 3. Cook the vegetables
steps: Increase the heat to medium-high. Add blanched cauliflower, carrot, and beans. Stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Add courgette and bell peppers. Toss to coat in the masala.
Splash in 2–3 tbsp hot water, cover, and cook for 3–4 minutes until just tender but still crisp.

instruction.title: 4. Finish & balance flavors
steps: Add garam masala and crushed fenugreek leaves. Stir gently.
If using paneer, add now and toss lightly.

instruction.title: 5. Serve
steps: Garnish with chopped coriander and serve hot with naan, roti, or jeera rice.
A squeeze of fresh lemon right before serving lifts the tangy notes.

chefTips: High heat stir-fry: The “jhalfrezi” style is essentially a quick toss over high heat to keep vegetables vibrant and slightly crisp.
Tomato balance: Fresh tomatoes give acidity, while tomato purée deepens umami.
Layer spices: Bloom whole spices in oil first, then ground spices in cooked onion–tomato masala for complexity.
Paneer option: For a richer dish, lightly fry paneer cubes until golden before adding at the end.
